skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem: What is the solution set of the equation √(3x + 4) + 2 = x?
rationale: Isolate the radical: √(3x + 4) = x − 2. Square both sides: 3x + 4 = x2 − 4x + 4, so x2 − 7x = 0, i.e. x(x − 7) = 0. The candidates are x = 0 and x = 7. Check x = 0: √4 + 2 = 2 + 2 = 4 ≠ 0, extraneous. Check x = 7: √25 + 2 = 5 + 2 = 7 ✓. The solution set is {7}.
